title: Mellow Russian Fish Sushi
description: A lazy Sunday Russian dish featuring mirin-hon and nigiri-shaping, this pescatarian-friendly recipe combines yeasty and glassy-jelly elements for a unique taste experience.
instructions: Prepare the mirin-hon sauce by whisking together mirin and hon dashi in a small bowl.
Cut the salmon fillet into small pieces and set aside.
Mix the yeast extract with a small amount of water to create a yeast paste.
Combine the cooked sushi rice with the yeast paste and mix well.
Cut the nori sheets into desired sizes for nigiri-shaping.
Shape the sushi rice mixture into small balls and wrap with nori sheets.
Place a small piece of salmon in the center of each sushi ball.
Dissolve the agar agar in water and bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Pour the agar agar mixture over the sushi balls to create a glassy-jelly coating.
Serve the Mellow Russian Fish Sushi chilled, garnished with mirin-hon sauce.
